title: Astroloma ciliatum
intro: Astroloma ciliatum, also known as the hairy greenhood orchid, is a rare and endangered orchid species native to Australia. Traditionally, the plant has been used by Indigenous Australians to treat a range of health conditions, including respiratory and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Astroloma ciliatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In modern herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tincture or infused in hot water to create a tea, although its availability and use are limited due to its rarity and conservation status.
taxonomy: Astroloma ciliatum is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. It is a type of heath, commonly found in Australia. As a member of the Ericaceae family, Astroloma ciliatum is part of a broader group of plants that are primarily found in the Northern Hemisphere, but have also been introduced to other regions.
